title: HTTP Servers in TypeScript: Node 24 and Express 5 Production APIs
description: TypeScript developers who can build robust HTTP clients and relational data systems and now need production-grade Node and Express origin services. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate node 24.18 lts, npm 12, typescript 7 native and typescript 6 compatibility toolchains, node http, express 5.2.1, zod 4.4, node-postgres 8.22, jose 6.2, http semantics, routing, middleware, validation, bodies, streaming, async context, cancellation, concurrency, postgresql, consistency, authentication, authorization, browser and proxy trust, webhooks, ssrf, smuggling defense, node:test, supertest, fuzzing, opentelemetry 1.43, profiling, openapi 3.2, deployment, draining, and recovery through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-typescript decision functions plus explicit node, express, listener, tls, postgresql, proxy, worker, load, and production transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative production service projects and a performance-based certification exam through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

competencies.statement: Define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, and recovery evidence for a service outcome before choosing endpoints.
misconceptions: A larger route inventory proves the server creates more value for its users.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, and recovery evidence for a service outcome before choosing endpoints.

competencies.statement: Pin Node 24.18 LTS, npm 12, architecture, package policy, lockfile, environment, and supported deployment assumptions as one reproducible runtime contract.
misconceptions: Any recent Node major behaves identically once TypeScript source compiles.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pin Node 24.18 LTS, npm 12, architecture, package policy, lockfile, environment, and supported deployment assumptions as one reproducible runtime contract.

competencies.statement: Separate the TypeScript 7 native CLI from the TypeScript 6 compatibility API and prove which tool owns checking, emission, editor APIs, and build gates.
misconceptions: The native TypeScript 7 compiler exposes every JavaScript compiler API provided by TypeScript 6.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate the TypeScript 7 native CLI from the TypeScript 6 compatibility API and prove which tool owns checking, emission, editor APIs, and build gates.

competencies.statement: Use Node type stripping only for erasable syntax while keeping type checking, path transformation, module resolution, and production emission explicit.
misconceptions: Node running a dot-ts file means it type-checked the program and honored tsconfig paths.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Node type stripping only for erasable syntax while keeping type checking, path transformation, module resolution, and production emission explicit.

competencies.statement: Prove a clean npm ci, type gate, test, build, artifact, and startup under a changed environment without warm local residue.
misconceptions: A successful developer-server start proves a clean production build is reproducible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove a clean npm ci, type gate, test, build, artifact, and startup under a changed environment without warm local residue.

competencies.statement: Trace call stack, timers, pending callbacks, poll, check, close callbacks, microtasks, and next-tick work without assuming one request owns one event-loop turn.
misconceptions: Node completes one HTTP request before beginning work for another request.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace call stack, timers, pending callbacks, poll, check, close callbacks, microtasks, and next-tick work without assuming one request owns one event-loop turn.

competencies.statement: Configure ESM, CommonJS interop, package exports, file extensions, and NodeNext resolution without relying on accidental development-loader behavior.
misconceptions: Changing import syntax alone converts every CommonJS dependency and runtime into ESM.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure ESM, CommonJS interop, package exports, file extensions, and NodeNext resolution without relying on accidental development-loader behavior.

competencies.statement: Parse, validate, normalize, redact, and freeze configuration once before acquiring resources or accepting traffic.
misconceptions: Environment variables become trustworthy typed values when an interface declares their intended shape.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse, validate, normalize, redact, and freeze configuration once before acquiring resources or accepting traffic.

competencies.statement: Sequence configuration, dependency creation, listener binding, liveness, readiness, background ownership, and atomic startup failure.
misconceptions: Listening on a port proves the service and every required dependency are ready.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Sequence configuration, dependency creation, listener binding, liveness, readiness, background ownership, and atomic startup failure.

competencies.statement: Handle termination signals with idempotent draining, bounded cleanup, rejection tracking, exit-code ownership, and forced-stop evidence.
misconceptions: Calling process.exit immediately after receiving SIGTERM gives pending responses time to finish.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle termination signals with idempotent draining, bounded cleanup, rejection tracking, exit-code ownership, and forced-stop evidence.

competencies.statement: Separate resource identity, server state, selected representation, transfer content, TypeScript model, and PostgreSQL rows.
misconceptions: A JSON value, TypeScript type, database row, and HTTP resource are necessarily the same thing.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate resource identity, server state, selected representation, transfer content, TypeScript model, and PostgreSQL rows.

competencies.statement: Apply safe, idempotent, and cacheable method properties independently and preserve them through application effects.
misconceptions: PUT and DELETE are safe because repeating them should have the same intended effect.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply safe, idempotent, and cacheable method properties independently and preserve them through application effects.

competencies.statement: Choose POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, status, Location, representation, and asynchronous completion behavior from the resource contract.
misconceptions: Every successful write returns status 200 and the complete updated object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, status, Location, representation, and asynchronous completion behavior from the resource contract.

competencies.statement: Use validators and If-Match, If-None-Match, dates, 304, 412, and 428 to prevent lost updates and unnecessary transfer.
misconceptions: ETags only optimize caches and cannot protect a mutation from stale client state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use validators and If-Match, If-None-Match, dates, 304, 412, and 428 to prevent lost updates and unnecessary transfer.

competencies.statement: Test methods against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, changed state, and durable effects.
misconceptions: One expected status assertion proves an endpoint obeys HTTP semantics.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test methods against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, changed state, and durable effects.

competencies.statement: Trace IncomingMessage and ServerResponse as streams with header commitment, body ownership, backpressure, abort, error, and close lifetimes.
misconceptions: A Node request and response are fully buffered plain objects owned by Express.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace IncomingMessage and ServerResponse as streams with header commitment, body ownership, backpressure, abort, error, and close lifetimes.

competencies.statement: Configure requestTimeout, headersTimeout, keepAliveTimeout, keepAliveTimeoutBuffer, maxHeadersCount, and maximum header size from a threat and latency budget.
misconceptions: The default Node HTTP timeouts are universally safe for slow clients and production traffic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure requestTimeout, headersTimeout, keepAliveTimeout, keepAliveTimeoutBuffer, maxHeadersCount, and maximum header size from a threat and latency budget.

competencies.statement: Distinguish HTTP/1.1, Node HTTP/2 compatibility behavior, ALPN, TLS, upgrades, CONNECT, and the explicit external boundary for HTTP/3.
misconceptions: Express automatically enables equivalent HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, and HTTP/3 behavior on one listener.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish HTTP/1.1, Node HTTP/2 compatibility behavior, ALPN, TLS, upgrades, CONNECT, and the explicit external boundary for HTTP/3.

competencies.statement: Separate server.close, closeIdleConnections, closeAllConnections, upgraded sockets, active responses, and forced shutdown.
misconceptions: server.close immediately terminates every active request and upgraded connection.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate server.close, closeIdleConnections, closeAllConnections, upgraded sockets, active responses, and forced shutdown.

competencies.statement: Require live TLS identity, proxy, slow-client, protocol negotiation, upgrade, drain, and packet-level evidence beyond deterministic browser models.
misconceptions: A pure request-decision function proves listener and wire-protocol behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require live TLS identity, proxy, slow-client, protocol negotiation, upgrade, drain, and packet-level evidence beyond deterministic browser models.

competencies.statement: Author Express 5.2 string paths with named wildcards, braces, reserved characters, and supported path-to-regexp syntax.
misconceptions: Express 4 wildcard, question-mark, and unnamed capture syntax behaves unchanged in Express 5.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Author Express 5.2 string paths with named wildcards, braces, reserved characters, and supported path-to-regexp syntax.

competencies.statement: Derive dispatch from registration order, method, path, router mounting, next-route behavior, and literal-versus-parameter overlap.
misconceptions: Express always selects the most specific matching route regardless of registration order.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive dispatch from registration order, method, path, router mounting, next-route behavior, and literal-versus-parameter overlap.

competencies.statement: Treat decoded string or wildcard-array parameters as untrusted input and validate identifier grammar, length, canonical form, and existence separately.
misconceptions: A matched route parameter is a valid domain identifier because Express decoded it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat decoded string or wildcard-array parameters as untrusted input and validate identifier grammar, length, canonical form, and existence separately.

competencies.statement: Reason about app, Router, mount paths, baseUrl, originalUrl, path, route, mergeParams, strict routing, and case sensitivity.
misconceptions: req.url, req.originalUrl, req.path, and req.baseUrl are interchangeable inside mounted routers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reason about app, Router, mount paths, baseUrl, originalUrl, path, route, mergeParams, strict routing, and case sensitivity.

competencies.statement: Enumerate literal, parameter, wildcard, HEAD, OPTIONS, 404, 405, encoded, and near-miss cases before accepting a route table.
misconceptions: If each intended endpoint succeeds once, routing is complete and conflict free.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enumerate literal, parameter, wildcard, HEAD, OPTIONS, 404, 405, encoded, and near-miss cases before accepting a route table.

competencies.statement: Compose request identity, trust, limits, parsing, authentication, authorization, application, error, and observation middleware in a defended order.
misconceptions: Middleware functions commute, so reordering them cannot change security or response behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compose request identity, trust, limits, parsing, authentication, authorization, application, error, and observation middleware in a defended order.

competencies.statement: Use Express 5 automatic Promise rejection forwarding while preserving causal error identity and avoiding duplicate next calls.
misconceptions: Every async Express handler still requires a catch wrapper that calls next manually.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Express 5 automatic Promise rejection forwarding while preserving causal error identity and avoiding duplicate next calls.

competencies.statement: Implement four-argument error middleware, normalized failure taxonomy, disclosure policy, and headersSent delegation without double writes.
misconceptions: An error handler with three parameters is recognized from its TypeScript annotation as error middleware.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement four-argument error middleware, normalized failure taxonomy, disclosure policy, and headersSent delegation without double writes.

competencies.statement: Construct long-lived dependencies at startup and inject narrow capabilities without request-time pools, mutable service locators, or hidden globals.
misconceptions: Creating a new database pool in every handler improves isolation and resource cleanup.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct long-lived dependencies at startup and inject narrow capabilities without request-time pools, mutable service locators, or hidden globals.

competencies.statement: Separate transport parsing, domain decisions, persistence, external effects, and serialization while assigning exactly one response owner.
misconceptions: A layered service requires many pass-through classes and lets any layer send the response.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate transport parsing, domain decisions, persistence, external effects, and serialization while assigning exactly one response owner.

competencies.statement: Model body, params, query, fields, configuration, token claims, database rows, and upstream data as unknown until runtime validation succeeds.
misconceptions: Express generic parameters make an incoming JSON body satisfy the declared TypeScript interface.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model body, params, query, fields, configuration, token claims, database rows, and upstream data as unknown until runtime validation succeeds.

competencies.statement: Use Zod 4 strict objects, safeParse, issue paths, bounds, unions, refinements, and error mapping without accepting undeclared properties.
misconceptions: A successful JSON parse rejects unknown keys and proves all domain invariants.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Zod 4 strict objects, safeParse, issue paths, bounds, unions, refinements, and error mapping without accepting undeclared properties.

competencies.statement: Order decoding, validation, canonicalization, authorization, and effect so normalization cannot change the resource or policy target after checks.
misconceptions: Normalization is harmless presentation cleanup that can occur after authorization.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Order decoding, validation, canonicalization, authorization, and effect so normalization cannot change the resource or policy target after checks.

competencies.statement: Construct narrow branded identifiers and domain values only through validated factories while avoiding unsafe assertions and impossible-state widening.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct narrow branded identifiers and domain values only through validated factories while avoiding unsafe assertions and impossible-state widening.

competencies.statement: Map domain results through explicit response schemas that minimize fields, stabilize nullability, and detect accidental secret or internal-property disclosure.
misconceptions: Returning a database row directly is safe when TypeScript knows its compile-time type.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map domain results through explicit response schemas that minimize fields, stabilize nullability, and detect accidental secret or internal-property disclosure.

competencies.statement: Interpret method, url, originalUrl, path, protocol, secure, hostname, host, ip, ips, headers, route, params, query, body, and socket using server-side semantics.
misconceptions: req.hostname, req.get host, req.ip, and socket.remoteAddress always describe the original client request.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret method, url, originalUrl, path, protocol, secure, hostname, host, ip, ips, headers, route, params, query, body, and socket using server-side semantics.

competencies.statement: Validate canonical host and port against configured authority before routing, link generation, cache keys, redirects, or tenant selection.
misconceptions: A syntactically valid Host header is trustworthy because Node required it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate canonical host and port against configured authority before routing, link generation, cache keys, redirects, or tenant selection.

competencies.statement: Configure trust proxy by explicit addresses or hop logic and derive client, protocol, and host only when every trusted hop overwrites forwarded fields.
misconceptions: Setting trust proxy to true always reveals the real client securely.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure trust proxy by explicit addresses or hop logic and derive client, protocol, and host only when every trusted hop overwrites forwarded fields.

competencies.statement: Traverse Forwarded or X-Forwarded chains from the trusted socket boundary and reject malformed, excessive, or topology-inconsistent claims.
misconceptions: The leftmost X-Forwarded-For entry always identifies the user agent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Traverse Forwarded or X-Forwarded chains from the trusted socket boundary and reject malformed, excessive, or topology-inconsistent claims.

competencies.statement: Record bounded route, request ID, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, personal data, or unbounded query values.
misconceptions: Logging originalUrl is safe because secrets appear only in authorization headers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record bounded route, request ID, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, personal data, or unbounded query values.

competencies.statement: Handle case-insensitive names, repeated lines, list grammar, whitespace, forbidden hop-by-hop fields, header size, and Node joining behavior by field semantics.
misconceptions: A comma split over req.headers values correctly parses every repeated HTTP field.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle case-insensitive names, repeated lines, list grammar, whitespace, forbidden hop-by-hop fields, header size, and Node joining behavior by field semantics.

competencies.statement: Validate Content-Type parameters and select an acceptable representation with quality, specificity, fallback, and 406 or 415 behavior.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate Content-Type parameters and select an acceptable representation with quality, specificity, fallback, and 406 or 415 behavior.

competencies.statement: Negotiate content coding, emit correct Vary, bound decoded size, avoid secret-dependent compression, and preserve streaming backpressure.
misconceptions: Compression changes only transfer size and therefore cannot affect security or caching.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Negotiate content coding, emit correct Vary, bound decoded size, avoid secret-dependent compression, and preserve streaming backpressure.

competencies.statement: Create stable strong or weak validators and apply read and write preconditions with correct precedence and representation scope.
misconceptions: Hashing JSON.stringify of an arbitrary object always yields a stable strong validator.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create stable strong or weak validators and apply read and write preconditions with correct precedence and representation scope.

competencies.statement: Define freshness, private versus shared storage, authorization interactions, stale behavior, invalidation, and CDN evidence for each route.
misconceptions: Private data is safe whenever the response omits an explicit public directive.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define freshness, private versus shared storage, authorization interactions, stale behavior, invalidation, and CDN evidence for each route.

competencies.statement: Configure express.json type, limit, inflate, strict, reviver, and verify behavior while treating req.body as undefined until parsing succeeds.
misconceptions: Express always parses JSON and req.body is an object even before body middleware runs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure express.json type, limit, inflate, strict, reviver, and verify behavior while treating req.body as undefined until parsing succeeds.

competencies.statement: Enforce declared, compressed, decoded, field-count, nesting, and semantic-size budgets before expensive parsing or effects.
misconceptions: A Content-Length limit bounds the decompressed body the application will allocate.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce declared, compressed, decoded, field-count, nesting, and semantic-size budgets before expensive parsing or effects.

competencies.statement: Preserve exact raw bytes for signature verification while separating timestamp, replay, media type, decode, validation, and enqueue decisions.
misconceptions: Parsing JSON and serializing it again produces the same bytes that a webhook provider signed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve exact raw bytes for signature verification while separating timestamp, replay, media type, decode, validation, and enqueue decisions.

competencies.statement: Bound URL-encoded parameters and stream multipart parts with filename, type, count, size, storage, scanning, cleanup, and path authority controls.
misconceptions: A multipart filename supplied by the client is a safe local storage path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound URL-encoded parameters and stream multipart parts with filename, type, count, size, storage, scanning, cleanup, and path authority controls.

competencies.statement: Stop reads and downstream work on abort, remove partial artifacts, record bounded causal evidence, and test slow, truncated, malformed, oversized, and changed bodies.
misconceptions: When the client disconnects, Node automatically rolls back every application effect and removes temporary files.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Stop reads and downstream work on abort, remove partial artifacts, record bounded causal evidence, and test slow, truncated, malformed, oversized, and changed bodies.

competencies.statement: Choose status and fields before commitment, assign one response owner, return after terminal sends, and observe writableEnded, headersSent, finish, close, and error distinctly.
misconceptions: A later res.status call replaces the status after headers or body bytes were sent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose status and fields before commitment, assign one response owner, return after terminal sends, and observe writableEnded, headersSent, finish, close, and error distinctly.

competencies.statement: Map stable internal failures to RFC 9457 types, status, title, detail, instance, extensions, localization, and disclosure policy.
misconceptions: The human-readable problem detail is a stable machine error code and may include internal diagnostics.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map stable internal failures to RFC 9457 types, status, title, detail, instance, extensions, localization, and disclosure policy.

competencies.statement: Control JSON nullability, undefined omission, BigInt, dates, non-finite numbers, property order, cycles, and serialization failure before commitment.
misconceptions: Any valid TypeScript value can be passed to res.json without loss or runtime failure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Control JSON nullability, undefined omission, BigInt, dates, non-finite numbers, property order, cycles, and serialization failure before commitment.

competencies.statement: Use stream pipeline, write backpressure, bounded buffers, cancellation, error ownership, trailers, and finish-versus-close evidence for large responses.
misconceptions: Calling response.write repeatedly cannot increase memory because Node streams always pause the producer automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use stream pipeline, write backpressure, bounded buffers, cancellation, error ownership, trailers, and finish-versus-close evidence for large responses.

competencies.statement: Serve downloads with authorized resource lookup, content disposition, safe filenames, media type, validators, ranges, HEAD parity, byte accounting, and abort cleanup.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Serve downloads with authorized resource lookup, content disposition, safe filenames, media type, validators, ranges, HEAD parity, byte accounting, and abort cleanup.

competencies.statement: Use AsyncLocalStorage run, enterWith limits, snapshot, name, and bounded request metadata without treating context as authorization.
misconceptions: AsyncLocalStorage values are safe global mutable state and prove the current user is authorized.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use AsyncLocalStorage run, enterWith limits, snapshot, name, and bounded request metadata without treating context as authorization.

competencies.statement: Compose client disconnect, service deadline, dependency timeout, and shutdown with AbortSignal.timeout and AbortSignal.any while preserving the first cause.
misconceptions: Promise.race stops losing asynchronous work after one promise settles.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compose client disconnect, service deadline, dependency timeout, and shutdown with AbortSignal.timeout and AbortSignal.any while preserving the first cause.

competencies.statement: Allocate request time among queueing, dependencies, response, and cleanup with monotonic evidence and no negative or expanding child budgets.
misconceptions: Every downstream operation should receive the full original request timeout.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Allocate request time among queueing, dependencies, response, and cleanup with monotonic evidence and no negative or expanding child budgets.

competencies.statement: Release clients, streams, files, locks, timers, listeners, and spans exactly once across success, rejection, abort, timeout, and shutdown.
misconceptions: Garbage collection promptly closes every external resource when a promise is abandoned.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Release clients, streams, files, locks, timers, listeners, and spans exactly once across success, rejection, abort, timeout, and shutdown.

competencies.statement: Own background promise lifetimes, observe rejection and multiple-resolution failures, fail safely, and distinguish crash recovery from request error handling.
misconceptions: A global unhandledRejection listener makes ignored promises safe to continue in production.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Own background promise lifetimes, observe rejection and multiple-resolution failures, fail safely, and distinguish crash recovery from request error handling.

competencies.statement: Distinguish asynchronous concurrency from parallelism and measure CPU blocking, synchronous I/O, long callbacks, and event-loop delay.
misconceptions: An async function automatically moves CPU-intensive work off the event-loop thread.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish asynchronous concurrency from parallelism and measure CPU blocking, synchronous I/O, long callbacks, and event-loop delay.

competencies.statement: Bound active requests, per-dependency concurrency, queue length, queue age, memory, rejection, Retry-After, and fairness separately.
misconceptions: A larger in-memory queue always increases availability during traffic bursts.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound active requests, per-dependency concurrency, queue length, queue age, memory, rejection, Retry-After, and fairness separately.

competencies.statement: Apply rate and quota policies to trusted identities, tenant and operation cost, distributed storage, clock behavior, headers, exemptions, and failure mode.
misconceptions: Rate limiting by req.ip alone fairly protects every API behind proxies.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply rate and quota policies to trusted identities, tenant and operation cost, distributed storage, clock behavior, headers, exemptions, and failure mode.

competencies.statement: Use worker threads for justified CPU work with bounded pools, structured clone or transfer ownership, cancellation, failure replacement, and shutdown.
misconceptions: Creating one worker thread per request is the safest and fastest CPU isolation strategy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use worker threads for justified CPU work with bounded pools, structured clone or transfer ownership, cancellation, failure replacement, and shutdown.

competencies.statement: Test saturation, noisy neighbors, dependency slowdown, queue shedding, readiness degradation, recovery time, and invariant preservation.
misconceptions: Returning some 503 responses proves the service has a complete overload strategy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test saturation, noisy neighbors, dependency slowdown, queue shedding, readiness degradation, recovery time, and invariant preservation.

competencies.statement: Create one bounded pg Pool per service role with connection, idle, acquisition, maximum-lifetime, error, statistics, readiness, and pool.end behavior.
misconceptions: A new Pool per request maximizes isolation and automatically prevents connection leaks.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create one bounded pg Pool per service role with connection, idle, acquisition, maximum-lifetime, error, statistics, readiness, and pool.end behavior.

competencies.statement: Use parameterized values, allowlisted identifiers, explicit columns, row validation, cardinality checks, timeouts, and cancellation without SQL string concatenation.
misconceptions: Template literals are safe SQL parameterization when values came through TypeScript types.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use parameterized values, allowlisted identifiers, explicit columns, row validation, cardinality checks, timeouts, and cancellation without SQL string concatenation.

competencies.statement: Acquire and release a client exactly once with finally while distinguishing acquisition, query, protocol, server, timeout, cancellation, and application failures.
misconceptions: A client borrowed from Pool is automatically released when its promise settles.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Acquire and release a client exactly once with finally while distinguishing acquisition, query, protocol, server, timeout, cancellation, and application failures.

competencies.statement: Run BEGIN, all statements, COMMIT or ROLLBACK on one client with isolation, constraints, locking, retries, and domain invariant evidence.
misconceptions: pool.query calls participate in one transaction after any client executes BEGIN.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run BEGIN, all statements, COMMIT or ROLLBACK on one client with isolation, constraints, locking, retries, and domain invariant evidence.

competencies.statement: Classify known commit, known rollback, and unknown commit outcomes and reconcile by operation identity instead of replaying uncertain effects.
misconceptions: A connection error during COMMIT proves the transaction rolled back and is safe to retry.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify known commit, known rollback, and unknown commit outcomes and reconcile by operation identity instead of replaying uncertain effects.

competencies.statement: Model resource states, legal transitions, commands, representations, invariants, tombstones, and conflict outcomes independently of table layout.
misconceptions: CRUD endpoints directly mirror database tables and therefore define a complete resource model.

competencies.statement: Scope idempotency keys to principal and operation, compare canonical request fingerprints, store outcome atomically, expire deliberately, and reject mismatches.

competencies.statement: Connect version columns, ETags, preconditions, atomic updates, affected-row checks, conflict disclosure, and safe retry behavior.
misconceptions: Reading a version and later comparing it in application code prevents concurrent lost updates.

competencies.statement: Design stable cursor ordering, tie breakers, direction, signed opaque cursors, filters, snapshot expectations, limits, and insert or delete behavior.
misconceptions: Offset pagination returns stable non-duplicated pages while rows are inserted or removed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design stable cursor ordering, tie breakers, direction, signed opaque cursors, filters, snapshot expectations, limits, and insert or delete behavior.

competencies.statement: Persist state and an outbox fact atomically, publish with deduplication and ordering assumptions, and reconcile stuck or duplicate delivery.
misconceptions: Publishing an event immediately after COMMIT makes state and message delivery atomic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Persist state and an outbox fact atomically, publish with deduplication and ordering assumptions, and reconcile stuck or duplicate delivery.

misconceptions: Hashing a password once with a fast general-purpose hash is sufficient secure storage.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply modern password hashing, salt, work policy, input bounds, constant-behavior comparison, breach and reset controls, rehash migration, and secret separation.

competencies.statement: Generate opaque session identifiers, store server-side state, rotate at authentication and privilege changes, expire idle and absolute lifetime, revoke, and prevent fixation.
misconceptions: A signed cookie containing all session data is automatically revocable and safe from fixation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Generate opaque session identifiers, store server-side state, rotate at authentication and privilege changes, expire idle and absolute lifetime, revoke, and prevent fixation.

competencies.statement: Use jose verification with explicit algorithms, trusted keys, issuer, audience, expiration, not-before, clock tolerance, token type, and bounded claim validation.
misconceptions: Base64-decoding a JWT and checking its exp claim authenticates the token.
